title: NOC 73401 – Printing press operators | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73401 professionals are the technical specialists responsible for the high-volume production of printed materials. As a TEER 3 occupation, Printing Press Operators set up and operate sheet and web-fed presses to print text and illustrations on diverse materials including paper, plastic, and metal. In 2026, as the industry transitions toward "Smart Print" automation and sustainable, bio-based inks, these operators are classified as Precision Manufacturing Talent. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws and the Ontario (OINP) Skilled Trades Stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group734 – Operators, utility maintenance and related workersMinor Group7340 – Operators, utility maintenance and related workersNOC Code73401

duties: Order Review: Review job orders to determine production time, color sequence, and quantities required to meet client specifications.
Plate Mounting: Mount plates or cylinders and make necessary mechanical adjustments to ensure proper ink transfer.
Ink Management: Fill ink fountains and take precise measurements to adjust settings for color control and viscosity.
Press Setup: Set up the press and check samples for ink coverage, alignment, and registration before starting the main production run.
Quality Monitoring: Monitor regular press runs for consistency using computer control consoles and make real-time adjustments as required.
Maintenance: Remove and clean plates and cylinders at the end of each press run; perform basic routine maintenance on machinery.
Crew Direction: Direct the activities of the press crew (feeders/assistants) and ensure all health and safety procedures are strictly followed.
Substrate Handling: Manage the feeding and tension of various substrates including paper, plastic, glass, and metal sheets.
Troubleshooting: Diagnose mechanical or digital malfunctions during high-speed runs and coordinate with maintenance technicians for repairs.
Final Inspection: Conduct final quality audits on finished products to ensure they meet the ISO printing standards required for commercial distribution.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Education: Completion of a college program in printing technology OR a combination of specialized high school/industry courses is usually required.
Training: Completion of a two-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to becoming a journey-level operator.
Certification: Trade certification is available but voluntary in all provinces except Quebec, where it is more regulated.
Experience: For those without formal education, several years of on-the-job training as a press assistant may be accepted by employers.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Flexographic or Rotogravure Operators in the packaging sector often earn annual salaries between $58,000 and $82,000.
job_outlook: The outlook is Balanced. While employment decline in traditional newsprint will lead to some position losses, Retirements will create over 10,000 openings nationally through 2033. The rapid growth of "Smart Packaging" (with integrated RFID or QR codes) will require operators to have stronger digital and IT competencies, ensuring long-term demand for tech-literate workers.
labour_market_demand: The demand for printing press operators in 2026 is Stable to Moderate, with a "Good" outlook in the Ottawa region and a "Moderate" outlook in the Toronto and Kingston regions. While digital media has reduced traditional newspaper volume, the Packaging and Labelling sector is seeing a record 12% growth due to e-commerce expansion. According to 2026 Job Bank data, approximately 55% of operators work in the printing support activities sector, while 16% support the automotive manufacturing supply chain (labels/manuals).
Advanced Technology Integration: Gain experience with the latest 8-color flexographic presses, high-speed digital inkjet systems, and 3D printing applications.
Manufacturing Stability: Beyond commercial print, these roles are critical to Canada's aerospace, automotive, and pharmaceutical packaging industries.
Superior Full-Time Opportunities: Over 91% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, significantly higher than the national average for technical trades.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC remains a recognized trade in 2026 Express Entry Category-Based draws, requiring 12 months of experience for the renewed Trade Occupations priority stream.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Renewed for 2026; requires 12 months of work experience (up from 6 months in 2025).
Ontario (OINP) Skilled Trades Stream: A frequent target for operators already working in Ontario’s extensive manufacturing hubs.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For operators with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: Priority for experienced operators with a job offer from a commercial printing or manufacturing company in British Columbia.
